campaign: BASALT Phase 3 — eval legs A-C (CPU), FINAL frozen engine

engine_build: cargo build --release --manifest-path <cam-basalt>/Cargo.toml; exit 0 in 5m28s; target/release/camelid.exe 17,066,496 B mtime 2026-07-17 07:18:31; no llama-*/camelid processes before build

sha_verifications.packs_method: protocol §3 sha256 pins are CRLF-rendered hashes, NOT raw committed bytes. Verified: raw bytes basic=45583dce40d86e82bc8911a016819adb89860ab8e114cb027b4fe0a47496b7ac deep=8d35db5816a6186becea1ba1a641c7136d7313537c680ecb287550080eb904c7 (worktree LF, no CRLF); LF->CRLF rendering of those exact bytes reproduces the §3 pins d9d54f57.../adcfef00... byte-for-byte; each pack has exactly 1 commit in history => content identity proven, hash dialect mismatch disclosed (method reused from prior run)

pin_tool.help_checked_once: all oracle-convention flags present in --help (no-repack, -fa, -ctk, -ctv, -ub, --temp, -no-cnv, --no-warmup, --no-display-prompt, --simple-io); no substitutions needed; --help exited cleanly (exit 0, no spin)
pin_tool.flag_notes: --no-display-prompt/--simple-io are display-only additions for clean subprocess capture; pin exposes no token ids in this mode - text-level capture with [end of text] EOS marker (stripped, recorded)

harness_notes: fresh --dump-step-logits dir per run (refuses non-empty dir); forced lists non-empty from legA emitted ids; CAMELID_SKIP_FIT_CHECK never needed (no fit-check balk on any run); harness stderr emits "[gemma4] token_ids: [...]" cross-checked 1:1 vs meta.json per-step argmax

safety.two_phase_pin_vs_camelid: Leg B pin phase (9 runs) fully exited and verified clean before the Camelid NVFP4-mm phase started; verified pre/post each phase
safety.sweeps: after every run, path-scoped kill-by-PID (cam-basalt/target or tools/llama-cpp paths only); zero leftovers swept all session; zero foreign processes seen
safety.timeouts: every run wrapped, 480s per-run cap, kill-by-PID on expiry (never fired)
safety.protocol_s6_tension: protocol §6 asks model-size+3GB free; Q8_0 legs ran at ~7.5-9.0GB free (<11.2GB). Applied the orchestrator-binding 4GB floor (G2 precedent); free RAM never below 7.49GB across the whole session - disclosed, all readings recorded

legA.positions_note: 296 actual vs 320 pack-cap: budgets are caps - capital-france EOS at 13/24, haiku-sea EOS at 19/32; all other prompts hit their limits
legA.determinism_note: capital-france rerun: stdout byte-identical, token ids identical, all 14 step_*.bin byte-identical
legA.oracle_fyi: 5/5 basic_v1 continuations match qa/gemma4/oracle 5d56eff pin (text+ids+stop_type) - FYI only, different pin
legA.nondisturbance_vs_shaE2: IDENTICAL - all 9 prompts token_ids byte-identical to prior shaE2 baseline (296/296 positions); the K-quant routing fix did NOT disturb the CPU Q8_0 path (non-disturbance claim holds)

legB.comparison_level: text (pin token ids not exposable in this capture mode; Camelid ids recorded from meta.json/stderr)
legB.normalization: CRLF->LF; pin [end of text] marker stripped; camelid prompt echo stripped; both sides rstripped (trailing-whitespace-only divergence would be masked - caveat recorded)

divergences.common_prefix: ...named Finn. Finn was a cheerful soul,

pin_next.note: pin token id inferred from Camelid top-32 membership (pin exposes no ids); 2462 decodes to the " always" seen in pin text

divergences.attribution: near-tie argmax flip: pin token (2462 " always") is Camelid top-2 at 0.084031 raw-logit gap; both full continuations recorded in legB_parity.json; top-32 retained in legB/camelid/dump-village-story/meta.json; NO tolerance widening applied (MUSTER probe-and-attribute discipline)

legB.note: reproduces prior shaE2 finding exactly (8/9, village-story step 13, gap 0.084); NVFP4 CPU path byte-behaviour unchanged by the K-quant fix

legC.regression_check: PASS - all 5 rows ran to completion (45/45 runs exit 0); the shaE2 K-quant panic (gemma4_runtime.rs:227 unreachable) is FIXED at this SHA; Q4K-mm, Q4_K_M-df, Q4_K_M-im all decoded cleanly
legC.sanity_q8: PASS 100.0 exactly (296/296); KL=0; 296/296 forced-run bins byte-identical to Leg A free-run bins
legC.kl_method: exact full-vocab (262144) KL(baseline_Q8_0 || R) per position, f64 log-softmax with max-shift, natural log; p95 = nearest-rank; per-position top-32 excerpts retained in each dump meta.json
legC.go_rule_note: NUMBERS ONLY reported; §5.2 GO rule NOT applied here (orchestrator applies verbatim); sanity guard agreement(Q4K-mm)=92.6 >= 80.0
legC.dumps: ALL step .bin dumps deleted after every row KL computed (per instruction); meta.json top-32 excerpts retained
